Tragacanth (Astragalus gummifer) is a plant that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is commonly known as Tragacanth or Ghatti gum. It is a shrub that produces a gummy sap, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine.

The health benefits of Tragacanth include soothing digestive issues, such as diarrhea and constipation, and reducing inflammation. It is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and sore throats. 

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include polysaccharides, glycoproteins, and other complex carbohydrates, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects.

The most common herbal preparations made with Tragacanth include lozenges, syrups, and capsules, which are used to treat a variety of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
